code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test17-NOV-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment with metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria despite normal serum glucose and low HbA1c, which may indicate tubular dysfunction rather than diabetes. Cloudy, brown urine with low specific gravity further supports renal involvement. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further workup for underlying renal pathology.
